name: pendulum
documentation:
# Pendulum

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pendulum.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pendulum
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pendulum.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pendulum
```

```{image} https://github.com/sdispater/pendulum/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Pendulum Build status
:target: https://github.com/sdispater/pendulum/actions
```

Python datetimes made easy.

Supports Python **3.8 and newer**.

```python
>>> import pendulum

>>> now_in_paris = pendulum.now('Europe/Paris')
>>> now_in_paris
'2016-07-04T00:49:58.502116+02:00'

# Seamless timezone switching
>>> now_in_paris.in_timezone('UTC')
'2016-07-03T22:49:58.502116+00:00'

>>> tomorrow = pendulum.now().add(days=1)
>>> last_week = pendulum.now().subtract(weeks=1)

>>> past = pendulum.now().subtract(minutes=2)
>>> past.diff_for_humans()
'2 minutes ago'

>>> delta = past - last_week
>>> delta.hours
23
>>> delta.in_words(locale='en')
'6 days 23 hours 58 minutes'

# Proper handling of datetime normalization
>>> pendulum.datetime(2013, 3, 31, 2, 30, tz='Europe/Paris')
'2013-03-31T03:30:00+02:00' # 2:30 does not exist (Skipped time)

# Proper handling of dst transitions
>>> just_before = pendulum.datetime(2013, 3, 31, 1, 59, 59, 999999, tz='Europe/Paris')
'2013-03-31T01:59:59.999999+01:00'
>>> just_before.add(microseconds=1)
'2013-03-31T03:00:00+02:00'
```

## Resources

- [Official Website](https://pendulum.eustace.io)
- [Documentation](https://pendulum.eustace.io/docs/)
- [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/sdispater/pendulum/issues)

## Why Pendulum?

Native `datetime` instances are enough for basic cases but when you face more complex use-cases
they often show limitations and are not so intuitive to work with.
`Pendulum` provides a cleaner and more easy to use API while still relying on the standard library.
So it's still `datetime` but better.

Unlike other datetime libraries for Python, Pendulum is a drop-in replacement
for the standard `datetime` class (it inherits from it), so, basically, you can replace all your `datetime`
instances by `DateTime` instances in your code (exceptions exist for libraries that check
the type of the objects by using the `type` function like `sqlite3` or `PyMySQL` for instance).

It also removes the notion of naive datetimes: each `Pendulum` instance is timezone-aware
and by default in `UTC` for ease of use.

Pendulum also improves the standard `timedelta` class by providing more intuitive methods and properties.

## Limitations

Even though the `DateTime` class is a subclass of `datetime` there are some rare cases where
it can't replace the native class directly. Here is a list (non-exhaustive) of the reported cases with
a possible solution, if any:

- `sqlite3` will use the `type()` function to determine the type of the object by default. To work around it you can register a new adapter:

```python
from pendulum import DateTime
from sqlite3 import register_adapter

register_adapter(DateTime, lambda val: val.isoformat(' '))
```

- `mysqlclient` (former `MySQLdb`) and `PyMySQL` will use the `type()` function to determine the type of the object by default. To work around it you can register a new adapter:

```python
import MySQLdb.converters
import pymysql.converters

from pendulum import DateTime

MySQLdb.converters.conversions[DateTime] = MySQLdb.converters.DateTime2literal
pymysql.converters.conversions[DateTime] = pymysql.converters.escape_datetime
```

- `django` will use the `isoformat()` method to store datetimes in the database. However since `pendulum` is always timezone aware the offset information will always be returned by `isoformat()` raising an error, at least for MySQL databases. To work around it you can either create your own `DateTimeField` or use the previous workaround for `MySQLdb`:

```python
from django.db.models import DateTimeField as BaseDateTimeField
from pendulum import DateTime


class DateTimeField(BaseDateTimeField):

    def value_to_string(self, obj):
        val = self.value_from_object(obj)

        if isinstance(value, DateTime):
            return value.to_datetime_string()

        return '' if val is None else val.isoformat()
```

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome, especially with localization.

### Getting started

To work on the Pendulum codebase, you'll want to clone the project locally
and install the required dependencies via [poetry](https://poetry.eustace.io).

```bash
$ git clone git@github.com:sdispater/pendulum.git
$ poetry install
```

### Localization

If you want to help with localization, there are two different cases: the locale already exists
or not.

If the locale does not exist you will need to create it by using the `clock` utility:

```bash
./clock locale create <your-locale>
```

It will generate a directory in `pendulum/locales` named after your locale, with the following
structure:

```text
<your-locale>/
    - custom.py
    - locale.py
```

The `locale.py` file must not be modified. It contains the translations provided by
the CLDR database.

The `custom.py` file is the one you want to modify. It contains the data needed
by Pendulum that are not provided by the CLDR database. You can take the [en](https://github.com/sdispater/pendulum/tree/master/pendulum/locales/en/custom.py)
data as a reference to see which data is needed.

You should also add tests for the created or modified locale.

DIFFERENCES

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.diff_for_humans
documentation:
Get the difference in a human readable format in the current locale.

When comparing a value in the past to default now:
1 day ago
5 months ago

When comparing a value in the future to default now:
1 day from now
5 months from now

When comparing a value in the past to another value:
1 day before
5 months before

When comparing a value in the future to another value:
1 day after
5 months after

:param other: The date to compare to (defaults to today)
:param absolute: removes time difference modifiers ago, after, etc
:param locale: The locale to use for localization

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.start_of
documentation:
Returns a copy of the instance with the time reset
with the following rules:

* day: time to 00:00:00
* week: date to first day of the week and time to 00:00:00
* month: date to first day of the month and time to 00:00:00
* year: date to first day of the year and time to 00:00:00
* decade: date to first day of the decade and time to 00:00:00
* century: date to first day of century and time to 00:00:00

:param unit: The unit to reset to
MODIFIERS

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.end_of
documentation:
Returns a copy of the instance with the time reset
with the following rules:

* week: date to last day of the week
* month: date to last day of the month
* year: date to last day of the year
* decade: date to last day of the decade
* century: date to last day of century

:param unit: The unit to reset to

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.next
documentation:
Modify to the next occurrence of a given day of the week.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the next occurrence
of the current day of the week.  Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

:param day_of_week: The next day of week to reset to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.previous
documentation:
Modify to the previous occurrence of a given day of the week.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the previous occurrence
of the current day of the week.  Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

:param day_of_week: The previous day of week to reset to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.first_of
documentation:
Returns an instance set to the first occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the first day of the unit.
Use the supplied consts to indicate the desired day_of_week,
ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

:param unit: The unit to use
:param day_of_week: The day of week to reset to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.last_of
documentation:
Returns an instance set to the last occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the last day of the unit.
Use the supplied consts to indicate the desired day_of_week,
ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

:param unit: The unit to use
:param day_of_week: The day of week to reset to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date.nth_of
documentation:
Returns a new instance set to the given occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If the calculated occurrence is outside the scope of the current unit,
then raise an error. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

:param unit: The unit to use
:param nth: The occurrence to use
:param day_of_week: The day of week to set to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._first_of_month
documentation:
Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the month. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

:param day_of_week: The day of week to set to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._last_of_month

Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the last day of the month. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

:param day_of_week: The day of week to set to.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._nth_of_month
documentation:
Modify to the given occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If the calculated occurrence is outside,
the scope of the current month, then return False and no
modifications are made. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._first_of_quarter

Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the quarter. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._last_of_quarter
documentation:
Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the last day of the quarter. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._nth_of_quarter
documentation:
Modify to the given occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If the calculated occurrence is outside,
the scope of the current quarter, then return False and no
modifications are made. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._first_of_year

Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current year. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the year. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. pendulum.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.date.Date._last_of_year

Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week

classes.name: pendulum.tz.timezone.Timezone

methods.name: pendulum.tz.timezone.Timezone.convert
documentation:
Converts a datetime in the current timezone.

If the datetime is naive, it will be normalized.

>>> from datetime import datetime
>>> from pendulum import timezone
>>> paris = timezone('Europe/Paris')
>>> dt = datetime(2013, 3, 31, 2, 30, fold=1)
>>> in_paris = paris.convert(dt)
>>> in_paris.isoformat()
'2013-03-31T03:30:00+02:00'

If the datetime is aware, it will be properly converted.

>>> new_york = timezone('America/New_York')
>>> in_new_york = new_york.convert(in_paris)
>>> in_new_york.isoformat()
'2013-03-30T21:30:00-04:00'

methods.name: pendulum.tz.timezone.Timezone.datetime
documentation: Return a normalized datetime for the current timezone.

Represents a named timezone.

The accepted names are those provided by the IANA time zone database.

>>> from pendulum.tz.timezone import Timezone
>>> tz = Timezone('Europe/Paris')

documentation: Create a DateTime instance for today.

documentation: Create a DateTime instance for tomorrow.

documentation: Create a DateTime instance for yesterday.

documentation: Creates a DateTime instance from a specific format.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.create

type.name: str | float | Timezone | FixedTimezone | None | datetime.tzinfo

documentation: Creates a new DateTime instance from a specific date and time.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.now
documentation: Get a DateTime instance for the current date and time.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.utcnow
documentation: Get a DateTime instance for the current date and time in UTC.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.set

type.name: str | float | Timezone | FixedTimezone | datetime.tzinfo | None

documentation: Getters/Setters

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.naive
documentation: Return the DateTime without timezone information.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.on
documentation: Returns a new instance with the current date set to a different date.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.at
documentation: Returns a new instance with the current time to a different time.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.in_timezone
documentation: Set the instance's timezone from a string or object.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.in_tz
documentation: Set the instance's timezone from a string or object.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_time_string
documentation: Format the instance as time.
STRING FORMATTING

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_datetime_string
documentation: Format the instance as date and time.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_day_datetime_string
documentation: Format the instance as day, date and time (in english).

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_atom_string
documentation: Format the instance as ATOM.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_cookie_string
documentation: Format the instance as COOKIE.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_iso8601_string
documentation: Format the instance as ISO 8601.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc822_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 822.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc850_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 850.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc1036_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 1036.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc1123_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 1123.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc2822_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 2822.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rfc3339_string
documentation: Format the instance as RFC 3339.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_rss_string
documentation: Format the instance as RSS.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.to_w3c_string
documentation: Format the instance as W3C.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._to_string
documentation: Format the instance to a common string format.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.closest
documentation: Get the closest date to the instance.
Comparisons

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.farthest
documentation: Get the farthest date from the instance.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.is_future
documentation: Determines if the instance is in the future, ie. greater than now.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.is_past
documentation: Determines if the instance is in the past, ie. less than now.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.is_long_year
documentation:
Determines if the instance is a long year

See link `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Week_dates`_

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.is_same_day
documentation:
Checks if the passed in date is the same day
as the instance current day.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.is_anniversary
documentation:
Check if its the anniversary.
Compares the date/month values of the two dates.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.add
documentation:
Add a duration to the instance.

If we're adding units of variable length (i.e., years, months),
move forward from current time, otherwise move forward from utc, for accuracy
when moving across DST boundaries.
ADDITIONS AND SUBSTRACTIONS

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.subtract
documentation: Remove duration from the instance.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._add_timedelta_
documentation: Add timedelta duration to the instance.
Adding a final underscore to the method name
to avoid errors for PyPy which already defines
a _add_timedelta method

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._subtract_timedelta
documentation: Remove timedelta duration from the instance.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.diff
documentation: Returns the difference between two DateTime objects represented as an Interval.
DIFFERENCES

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.diff_for_humans
documentation:
Get the difference in a human readable format in the current locale.

When comparing a value in the past to default now:
1 day ago
5 months ago

When comparing a value in the future to default now:
1 day from now
5 months from now

When comparing a value in the past to another value:
1 day before
5 months before

When comparing a value in the future to another value:
1 day after
5 months after

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.start_of
documentation:
Returns a copy of the instance with the time reset
with the following rules:

* second: microsecond set to 0
* minute: second and microsecond set to 0
* hour: minute, second and microsecond set to 0
* day: time to 00:00:00
* week: date to first day of the week and time to 00:00:00
* month: date to first day of the month and time to 00:00:00
* year: date to first day of the year and time to 00:00:00
* decade: date to first day of the decade and time to 00:00:00
* century: date to first day of century and time to 00:00:00
Modifiers

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.end_of
documentation:
Returns a copy of the instance with the time reset
with the following rules:

* second: microsecond set to 999999
* minute: second set to 59 and microsecond set to 999999
* hour: minute and second set to 59 and microsecond set to 999999
* day: time to 23:59:59.999999
* week: date to last day of the week and time to 23:59:59.999999
* month: date to last day of the month and time to 23:59:59.999999
* year: date to last day of the year and time to 23:59:59.999999
* decade: date to last day of the decade and time to 23:59:59.999999
* century: date to last day of century and time to 23:59:59.999999

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_second
documentation: Reset microseconds to 0.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_second
documentation: Set microseconds to 999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_minute
documentation: Reset seconds and microseconds to 0.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_minute
documentation: Set seconds to 59 and microseconds to 999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_hour
documentation: Reset minutes, seconds and microseconds to 0.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_hour
documentation: Set minutes and seconds to 59 and microseconds to 999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_day
documentation: Reset the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_day
documentation: Reset the time to 23:59:59.999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_month
documentation: Reset the date to the first day of the month and the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_month
documentation:
Reset the date to the last day of the month
and the time to 23:59:59.999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_year
documentation: Reset the date to the first day of the year and the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_year
documentation:
Reset the date to the last day of the year
and the time to 23:59:59.999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_decade
documentation:
Reset the date to the first day of the decade
and the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_decade
documentation:
Reset the date to the last day of the decade
and the time to 23:59:59.999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_century
documentation:
Reset the date to the first day of the century
and the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_century
documentation:
Reset the date to the last day of the century
and the time to 23:59:59.999999.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._start_of_week
documentation:
Reset the date to the first day of the week
and the time to 00:00:00.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._end_of_week
documentation:
Reset the date to the last day of the week
and the time to 23:59:59.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.next
documentation:
Modify to the next occurrence of a given day of the week.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the next occurrence
of the current day of the week.  Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.previous
documentation:
Modify to the previous occurrence of a given day of the week.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the previous occurrence
of the current day of the week.  Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.first_of
documentation:
Returns an instance set to the first occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the first day of the unit.
Use the supplied consts to indicate the desired day_of_week,
ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.last_of
documentation:
Returns an instance set to the last occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If no day_of_week is provided, modify to the last day of the unit.
Use the supplied consts to indicate the desired day_of_week,
ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.nth_of
documentation:
Returns a new instance set to the given occurrence
of a given day of the week in the current unit.
If the calculated occurrence is outside the scope of the current unit,
then raise an error. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

Supported units are month, quarter and year.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._first_of_month
documentation:
Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the month. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._last_of_month
documentation:
Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the last day of the month. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._nth_of_month
documentation:
Modify to the given occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current month. If the calculated occurrence is outside,
the scope of the current month, then return False and no
modifications are made. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._first_of_quarter
documentation:
Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the quarter. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._last_of_quarter
documentation:
Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the last day of the quarter. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._nth_of_quarter
documentation:
Modify to the given occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current quarter. If the calculated occurrence is outside,
the scope of the current quarter, then return False and no
modifications are made. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._first_of_year
documentation:
Modify to the first occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current year. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the first day of the year. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._last_of_year
documentation:
Modify to the last occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current year. If no day_of_week is provided,
modify to the last day of the year. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime._nth_of_year
documentation:
Modify to the given occurrence of a given day of the week
in the current year. If the calculated occurrence is outside,
the scope of the current year, then return False and no
modifications are made. Use the supplied consts
to indicate the desired day_of_week, ex. DateTime.MONDAY.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.average
documentation:
Modify the current instance to the average
of a given instance (default now) and the current instance.

methods.name: pendulum.datetime.DateTime.fromtimestamp
documentation: Native methods override
